content.full_text:
November 30, 1977

Martha Sillman, Treasurer
Post Office Box 3875
Granada Hills, California 91344

Dear Ms. Sillman:

All candidates for state and local office are notified at the
time they take out their nomination papers that a Statement of
Economic Interest (financial disclosure) is due at the same
time they file their Declaration of Candidacy. Elected candidates are notified by local or state officials as to when
additional financial disclosure is required. Candidates who
are not elected have no obligation to file another Statement
of Economic Interest.

Campaign disclosure forms which include all money raised and
spent in connection with the candidacy are filed with state or
local filing officers, depending on the office sought. Candidates for city offices file with the city clerk, county candidates with the county clerk and state or statewide candidates
with the Secretary of State, P.O. Box 1467, Sacramento, CA
95807. Notice of filing deadlines should be obtained from
filing officers along with necessary campaign disclosure forms.

Enclosed for your use, however, are some general filing deadlines for the March municipal and June and November state
elections.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

RSK:bw

LAW OFFICES

WILLIAM S. GREENE

MAILING ADDRESS:
P.O. BOX 3875
SUITE 107
17715 CHATSWORTH STREET
GRANADA HILLS, CALIFORNIA 91344
(213) 363-5694

November 23, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California    95814

Re:  Committee to Elect Bill Greene
     Post Office Box 33444
     Granada Hills, California 91344

Gentlemen:

Please let us know whether we will be notified when the Financial Statement or any other forms are due.

If no notification will be mailed, please let us know by return mail.

Sincerely yours,

[signature]

MARTHA SILLMAN
Treasurer

MS:gn

sections.question: Please let us know whether we will be notified when the Financial Statement or any other forms are due. If no notification will be mailed, please let us know by return mail.
sections.conclusion: All candidates for state or local office are notified at the time they file their nomination papers that a Statement of Economic Interest (financial disclosure) is due at the same time they file their Declaration of Candidacy. Elected candidates are notified by local or state officials as to when additional financial disclosure is required. Candidates who are not elected have no obligation to file another Statement of Economic Interest. Campaign disclosure reports, which include all money raised and spent in connection with the candidacy, are filed with state or local filing officers depending on the office sought.
sections.question_synthetic: Will the candidate committee receive notification of when financial disclosure statements and other required forms are due?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Candidates receive notification of financial disclosure requirements when filing nomination papers and their Declaration of Candidacy. Elected candidates receive additional notifications from local or state officials, while non-elected candidates have no further filing obligations. Campaign disclosure reports must be filed with appropriate state or local officers.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (from Martha Sillman, Treasurer) and FPPC response (from Regina Siciliano-Kutchins). The response letter lacks explicit "QUESTION" and "CONCLUSION" headers but the question and conclusion content are clearly identifiable. Some text appears degraded or contains OCR errors (e.g., 'seaee' for 'state', 'eehe' for 'the', 'eheir' for 'their'). Handwritten date at top shows November 30, 1977.

QUESTION: Please let us know whether we will be notified when the Financial Statement or any other forms are due. If no notification will be mailed, please let us know by return mail.

CONCLUSION: All candidates for state or local office are notified at the time they file their nomination papers that a Statement of Economic Interest (financial disclosure) is due at the same time they file their Declaration of Candidacy. Elected candidates are notified by local or state officials as to when additional financial disclosure is required. Candidates who are not elected have no obligation to file another Statement of Economic Interest. Campaign disclosure reports, which include all money raised and spent in connection with the candidacy, are filed with state or local filing officers depending on the office sought.

Elected candidates are notified by local or state officials as to when additional financial disclosure is required. Candidates who are not elected have no obligation to file another Statement of Economic Interest. Campaign disclosure forms which include all money raised and spent in connection with the candidacy are filed with state or local filing officers, depending on the office sought. Candidates for city offices file with the city clerk, county candidates with the county clerk and state or statewide candidates with the Secretary of State, P.O. Box 1467, Sacramento, CA 95807. embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter responds to a candidate committee's inquiry about notification of financial disclosure filing deadlines. The FPPC confirms that candidates are notified of disclosure requirements when they file their nomination papers and provides guidance on where to file campaign disclosure reports.
